name: Texas Bank
description_short: Texas Bank is a community-focused FDIC-insured bank offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services across multiple Texas locations.
description_long: Texas Bank operates as a full-service community bank with FDIC insurance backing, positioning itself around local decision-making and personalized service. The bank serves both personal and business customers through physical branches in Texas cities including Frisco, Tyler, Longview, and Prosper, emphasizing accessibility and community commitment. Their service portfolio spans traditional banking products including checking accounts (with specialized tiers for students, seniors, and public servants), savings accounts, certificates of deposit, IRAs, and money market accounts for personal customers. For business clients, Texas Bank offers business checking, business savings, business money market accounts, business lines of credit, and specialized lending including SBA loans, agriculture loans, and commercial real estate financing. The bank distinguishes itself through digital banking capabilities including Apple Pay and Google Pay integration, surcharge-free ATM access, remote deposit capture for business customers, and the MyCardRules debit card app for transaction control. Additionally, they offer mortgage services, home equity loans, personal loans, auto loans, and recreational vehicle loans. Texas Bank also provides an insurance division (Texas Insurance Center, licensed in Texas) for personal and commercial coverage, alongside cash management and merchant card services for business clients. However, the company operates with a limited geographic footprint confined to select Texas markets, which may restrict accessibility for customers outside their service areas. Their website provides standard banking information but limited detail on competitive rates, fee structures, or comprehensive product comparisons versus larger regional or national institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts (Texas Reward Checking, Value Checking, Interest Checking, Public Servant Checking, Senior Checking, Student Checking)
Personal savings accounts and student savings accounts
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) and Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs)
Business checking and business savings accounts
Personal and business loans including auto loans and recreational vehicle loans
Mortgage loans, home equity loans, and residential construction loans
SBA loans, agriculture loans, and commercial real estate loans
Digital banking with Apple Pay and Google Pay integration
Remote deposit capture and merchant card services for businesses
Rewards debit cards with MyCardRules app
Insurance products through Texas Insurance Center
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. government guarantee
Digital banking tools including Apple Pay, Google Pay, and remote deposit capture
Specialized checking accounts for specific demographics (students, seniors, public servants)
Comprehensive loan offerings including SBA, agriculture, and commercial real estate
MyCardRules debit card app providing transaction controls and monitoring
Integrated insurance services through Texas Insurance Center subsidiary
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to select Texas markets (Frisco, Tyler, Longview, Prosper)
No published rates, fees, or detailed product comparisons visible on website
Insurance products not FDIC-insured and not guaranteed by the bank
Website security warning about outdated browser vulnerabilities may reduce customer confidence
Limited information about minimum account balances, overdraft fees, or account maintenance costs
best_for: Texas residents in Frisco, Tyler, Longview, Prosper and surrounding areas seeking local banking relationships
Small business owners requiring SBA loans, agriculture financing, or cash management services
Customers prioritizing FDIC protection and community-focused banking over national institutions
diagnosis: Texas Bank is best suited for Texas residents within their service areas (Frisco, Tyler, Longview, Prosper) who value community banking, local decision-making, and FDIC-insured products. The main caveat is the limited geographic availability and lack of published rate/fee transparency on their website, which limits comparison shopping and may require direct contact to evaluate competitive positioning.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Community-focused FDIC-insured bank offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services across Texas.
